introSection: Sprinter was a steel kiddie roller coaster built by Dutch manufacturer J. Bakker Denies that was previously located at Clacton Pier in Clacton-on-Sea, Essex, England, UK. It was said to have been exported to Poland following its stay on the pier.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The Sprinter used drive tyres to propel the train backwards and forwards through a figure-eight shaped layout.

fullText:
Sprinter was a steel kiddie roller coaster built by Dutch manufacturer J. Bakker Denies that was previously located at Clacton Pier in Clacton-on-Sea, Essex, England, UK. It was said to have been exported to Poland following its stay on the pier.

The Sprinter used drive tyres to propel the train backwards and forwards through a figure-eight shaped layout.
